sections.paragraph:
μιμνῄσϰω [cp. Hom. μέμονα ‘bear in mind’, also μνάομαι in sense ‘be mindful of’; ‘remind’] mid. μιμνῄσϰομαι ‘call something to mind that one has noted or thought about in the past’

—a.recollect, remember Mt 5:23; 27:63; Lk 16:25; 24:6; freq. w. gen. Mt 26:75; Lk 24:8; J 2:22; Ac 11:16; 1 Cor 11:2; 2 Ti 1:4; Hb 13:3; 2 Pt 3:2.

—b. when it appears that the protagonist in the narrative has forgotten, μ. takes on the nuance think of, be mindful of Lk 1:72; 23:42; Hb 2:6; 8:12. The same idea is expressed in the pass. Ac 10:31; Rv 16:9.
